_comment: IDENTITY.json — Brahma's capability-certificate (doc 13 §13.5). This is the on-disk IdentityRecord the deterministic Yama gate and Kaal-Bhairav boundary actually read; SOUL/INSTRUCTIONS prose is NOT authoritative against it. INVARIANT region is boot-gated and editable only at top-gate C/D; VARIABLE region edits route through tiered reversibility, with any new grant or taint-clearance widening paying the full GLR + Sequencer cost.

bound_toolset.names_constraint_relaxed: may read an inbound goal/mission envelope from Shiva/Brihaspati/Hanuman, restrained to trusted:* labelled inputs; quarantined:* content is DATA only
bound_toolset.falsifier: if a read returns quarantined:* or embedded-instruction content acted upon as a command, the grant is mis-scoped and must be revoked

bound_toolset.names_constraint_relaxed: may read the seed-role roster + capability-commons embedding index to avoid drafting redundant roles, restrained to metadata/contract reads (no skill execution, no secret read)
bound_toolset.falsifier: if this grant ever reads a secret, executes a commons skill, or writes the commons, it exceeds its restraint

bound_toolset.names_constraint_relaxed: may emit a TypedPlan+RoleManifest as a WorkerOutputEnvelope with disposition=PROPOSAL under optimistic-veto + timelock; restrained to proposing only — never dispatch, route, execute, or reduce
bound_toolset.falsifier: if an emitted envelope ever has disposition != PROPOSAL, or directly triggers an effect without passing Shiva-routing + the Yama gate, the grant is mis-scoped

bound_toolset.names_constraint_relaxed: may address a RoleCharter request to the Role-Charterer for an uncovered specialization (stub, risk_ceiling A, ZERO grants); restrained to requesting — never minting, signing, or granting a role/capability
bound_toolset.falsifier: if Brahma ever mints a role, signs a triad, or attaches a non-zero grant to a charter, the grant is exceeded

bound_toolset.names_constraint_relaxed: may emit plan-lifecycle events for Chitragupta to hash-chain; restrained to EMITTING events only — Brahma never writes the audit fabric itself (Chitragupta is exclusive writer)
bound_toolset.falsifier: if Brahma ever writes directly to any audit store rather than emitting an event for Chitragupta, the boundary is breached

_capability_notes.least_privilege_rationale: Brahma's effects are strictly read-and-propose. Every write-direction effect is capped: emit a PROPOSAL (never an action), request a charter (never mint a role), emit audit events (never write the audit log). No external-network, artifact-mutation, secret-read, dispatch, enforce, halt, or audit-write effect appears in the bound_toolset — those belong to Tvastr/Agni, governance VCs, Shiva, Yama, Vishnu, and Chitragupta respectively.
_capability_notes.taint_clearance_direction: max_read_confidentiality is held at internal goal/roster level and min_write_integrity at proposal:unratified — Brahma's output is explicitly low-integrity (a proposal, not a ratified action), which is the safe direction. Any future RAISING of read-confidentiality or LOWERING of write-integrity is a gate-loosening that must pay the full doc 03 §7 GLR + §10 Sequencer cost; only tightening is a free Class-A append.
_capability_notes.risk_ceiling_rationale: ceiling=B matches the role brief: Brahma proposes (optimistic-veto + timelock) and never self-acts above B. Any plan node that would itself reach C/D is proposed-and-held for human approval/authorization, never self-resolved.
